svg.title: start.sh Runtime Modes
svg.desc: Current start.sh command modes and how they map to Docker Compose behavior.
svg.text: `start.sh` (Current): Command Modes and Runtime Effect
Baseline (applies to all modes)
`start.sh` no longer has `dev`/`prod` branching. It selects Docker Compose invocation (`docker compose` or `docker-compose`).
Compose stack includes `tools`, `homeagent`, and `cloudflared`; `homeagent` runs with `APP_ENV=production` and webhook ingress.
Core app behavior (agent/context/policy/scheduler) is defined in Python services, not start.sh mode switches.
Mode Matrix
`./start.sh up` (default)
Runs: `docker compose build && docker compose up -d`
Effect: builds images, starts stack detached, prints follow-up commands (`logs`, `stop`).
Operational result: webhook endpoint and admin port come online after healthchecks.
`./start.sh logs`
Runs: `docker compose logs -f`
Effect: attaches to live service logs for all compose services; no topology/state change.
`./start.sh stop`
Runs: `docker compose down`
Effect: stops and removes running containers/network for the stack.
`./start.sh restart`
Runs: `docker compose down` then `docker compose build && docker compose up -d`
Effect: full rebuild and clean re-launch of all services.
Useful after dependency/config/prompt changes that need new container startup state.
Message-processing logic remains identical after restart (webhook -> bot -> context -> agent -> persistence).
Note: file name retained for continuity, but content now documents actual start.sh behavior rather than obsolete dev/prod split.
